tier_remaining_note: These four fields drift from the live generate_build_plan_v2.py scoring model (a fresh recount on 2026-07-13 found A=12, B=746, C=7171, D=24631 remaining) and are not covered by verify_build_state.py's disk reconciliation, only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are. tier_b_remaining above is only decremented by this chunk's 25 routes from its prior stored value, not recomputed from scratch. A future maintenance pass should add tier-remaining reconciliation to verify_build_state.py so these four fields stop drifting silently.
notes: Chunk 81 DONE (Template A, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block). Built via 14 parallel research agents. Delivered the United States side of the reciprocal cluster opened in chunk 80: the same 12 countries (Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Russia, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine, Venezuela) each got united-states-to-X.md (12 routes); 11 of the 12 (all but Bolivia, since bolivia-to-united-states.md already existed) also got X-to-united-states.md (11 routes) = 23 routes, plus canada-to-maldives.md and brunei-to-canada.md (2 routes, genuinely new pairs, both directions previously unbuilt) = 25 routes. CDC high-risk-for-dog-rabies status independently verified per country against cdc.gov/importation/dogs/high-risk-countries.html (list dated 15 April 2026): El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Tunisia, Russia, Ukraine and Venezuela are HIGH RISK (FAVN titre test, CDC Dog Import Form, entry restricted to one of only six CDC-registered animal care facility airports: Atlanta, JFK, LAX, Miami, Philadelphia, Washington Dulles); Fiji, Nicaragua, Paraguay and Serbia are NOT high risk (standard CDC Dog Import Form only, no titre test), correcting a prior working assumption that Paraguay would be high risk. Genuine differentiators found: Fiji has a real asymmetry, no US-side quarantine or titre leaving Fiji, but a mandatory minimum 30-day BAF quarantine at Koronivia for a US-origin pet entering Fiji, since that is Fiji's own domestic biosecurity rule and applies regardless of origin. Russia and Ukraine both have no direct US flights for reasons distinct from their EU/UK routing problems: the FAA/US DOT blocked Russian aircraft from US airspace on 2 March 2022 (a separate US legal order from the EU/UK sanctions used on the chunk 80 pages), routed via Istanbul, Dubai or Doha; Ukraine's closed civilian airspace (since 24 February 2022, still closed per EASA's Conflict Zone Information Bulletin as of mid-2026) means a flight only reaches Warsaw, Krakow, Rzeszow or a Romanian city, with the final leg into Ukraine done overland by car. Venezuela's flight situation changed materially since chunk 80's UK pages were written: twin magnitude ~7.1-7.5 earthquakes struck northern Venezuela on 24 June 2026, structurally damaging Simon Bolivar International Airport (Caracas) and closing it to scheduled commercial traffic; airlines have rerouted through Valencia's Arturo Michelena airport instead (Avianca, Copa, Iberia, Air Europa), and American Airlines had to suspend its just-resumed (April 2026) nonstop Miami-Caracas service again through at least 10 July 2026 with no confirmed resumption date, superseding the older 'American Eagle cancellations' narrative used on the Venezuela-UK pages. El Salvador: found a real CDC-compliant workaround to the American Airlines dog cargo embargo established in chunk 80, Avianca and Delta both fly nonstop from San Salvador directly into CDC-registered facility airports; also confirmed USDA APHIS eFile endorsement is explicitly NOT required for pets exported from the US to El Salvador, an exception to the standard ~USD 38 pattern used on every other US-origin page. Guatemala: dogs also need a New World Screwworm-affected-country certificate (2026 APHIS/CDC NWS designation, alongside Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama) confirming a screwworm-free inspection within 5 days of shipment, layered on top of the CDC titre pathway; a MAGA import permit must be secured before a US-origin pet leaves. Honduras: United Airlines flies both San Pedro Sula and Palmerola nonstop to Houston, but Houston has no CDC-registered animal care facility, so that specific routing fails CDC entry rules for a dog even though the flight itself is real and convenient; American (Miami) and Delta (Atlanta) work instead. Nicaragua: the US export certificate must be ink-signed, embossed and mailed by USDA rather than endorsed electronically via the standard APHIS eFile system. Serbia: Air Serbia flies Belgrade-JFK direct and, unlike its blanket UK/UAE/Qatar pet exclusion, does carry pets cabin-only on this specific route (8kg cap, roughly EUR 130); could not confirm whether the US sits on Serbia's own favourable-risk import tier, stated as an open question per the YMYL guard rather than guessed. Tunisia: confirmed CDC high-risk, and the existing Pit Bull/Rottweiler/Tosa/Mastiff-type breed ban applies regardless of a pet's US origin. Bolivia: found the Bolivian Consulate in Washington DC requires an accompanying USD 85 money order, not a card payment, to legalise the export certificate. Canada-to-Maldives: paired Canada's CFIA export process (accredited vet plus CFIA endorsement, roughly CAD 20-40) with the Maldives' established dog-import ban and cats-only pathway, routed via Doha, Dubai or Abu Dhabi from Toronto. Brunei-to-Canada (a genuinely new pair, previously unbuilt in either direction): confirmed Royal Brunei Airlines carries no unaccompanied pet cargo on any route, so routing transits Singapore or Hong Kong; confirmed Brunei is not on CFIA's high-risk list, so no titre test applies on the Canada import side, a lighter pathway than both the CDC-titre US sibling route and the UK's titre-plus-90-day-wait route. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, correct Marcus Webb persona, valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block, all route_data fields populated, no placeholder strings, no contact email or phone stated beyond legitimately-sourced government agency contacts already established on sibling pages, every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk with a Python script, opening sentences independently varied and fact-led rather than a templated 'Moving a pet from X to Y' skeleton). Routes 6,446 -> 6,471, pages 7,252 -> 7,277. Chunk 82 (Template B, Tier B) is next. Content plan Day 32 (UK to South Africa route page) reconciliation check remains outstanding from chunk 80, not addressed this run.
_previous_notes_chunk_80: Chunk 80 DONE (Template E, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block). Also fixed a bookkeeping bug found at the start of this run: chunk 79 (Brunei/Laos/Seychelles/Trinidad and Tobago/Uzbekistan EU coverage + Macau UK/US/Australia gaps, routes 6,396 -> 6,421) was already committed to main (c890e545) with its route/page counts correctly bumped, but completed_chunks and next_chunk were left at 78/79 instead of advancing to 79/80, and the notes field was never rewritten to describe chunk 79's own content, only carried the stale chunk-78 narrative forward. Cross-checked against BUILD-PLAN.md (which correctly showed 'Chunks 1-79 complete, Chunk 80 next') and git log before treating chunk 80 as the correct next unit, rather than either re-building already-shipped chunk 79 content or skipping this run under the false premise that nothing was left to build. completed_chunks/next_chunk are now 80/81, correctly reflecting reality. Chunk 80 closed reciprocal UK gaps for 12 Tier B countries with no prior UK coverage in either direction: Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Russia, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine and Venezuela (24 routes, both directions each) plus Bolivia-to-United-States (1 route) = 25 routes, researched via 12 parallel web-search agents, one per country. Genuine differentiators found: Russia and Fiji are in fact GB-listed (low-risk) for pet entry, contrary to the working assumption going into this chunk, so their UK-bound pages need no titre test or 3-month wait, only the standard 21-day post-vaccination wait; verified directly against gov.uk's primary listed-country pages rather than assumed from third-party aggregators. Russia-UK and Ukraine-UK both have no direct flight: Russian airspace has been closed to UK/EU aircraft and Russian carriers banned from UK/EU airspace since 2022 with no change confirmed for 2026, so Russia routing goes via Istanbul or Dubai; Ukrainian civilian airspace has been closed to all commercial flights since 24 February 2022 and remains closed as of July 2026 despite an EASA-tracked phased-reopening working group formed in March 2026, so Ukraine routing goes overland to Poland or Romania before flying. The UK's Ukraine Family Scheme / Homes for Ukraine pet provisions are still active in 2026 but only add a licence-application fallback path (up to 5 pets, possible home isolation instead of kennel quarantine), they do not relax the underlying titre-test/vaccination biosecurity requirements, a distinction both Ukraine pages state explicitly rather than implying a blanket exemption. Fiji's own import rules for pets arriving from the UK require a mandatory minimum 30-day quarantine at a BAF facility (Fiji does not treat the UK as low-risk even though the UK treats Fiji as low-risk), so quarantine_required is true only on the united-kingdom-to-fiji page, correctly asymmetric. Venezuela's international flight links remain genuinely volatile (Copa suspended Caracas flights over safety concerns; American Eagle cancelled seven flights in three days in July 2026), so the Venezuela pages recommend Avianca's Bogota-Heathrow connection as the most consistently available routing while stating plainly that availability should be reconfirmed close to travel rather than asserted as guaranteed. El Salvador is CDC high-risk for canine rabies, which has led American Airlines Cargo to embargo new dog cargo bookings from that origin (cats unaffected), a dog-specific complication unique to that route. Serbia's own Air Serbia flies Belgrade-Heathrow direct roughly ten times weekly but excludes all pet travel on UK, UAE and Qatar routes under its own policy, so the obvious direct flight is unusable and both Serbia pages route via Vienna or Frankfurt instead. Tunisia and Paraguay, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua all confirmed unlisted for GB entry (full titre-test pathway), each with a named export authority and a genuine local edge case (Tunisia's own breed ban on Pit Bull/Rottweiler/Tosa/Mastiff-crosses; Paraguay's 5-pet cap and separate breed ban; Guatemala's OIRSA USD 14 port-inspection fee; Nicaragua's non-physical 'quarantine permit' that is administrative paperwork, not confinement). All 25 files QA-scanned clean after a second manual pass caught and fixed three issues the agents' own self-checks missed: two files (paraguay-to-united-kingdom.md, united-kingdom-to-paraguay.md) had template_variant set to B and D respectively instead of this chunk's E, one broken link (a non-existent /pet-transport/countries/tunisia/ country guide, corrected to the UK origin hub), and one banned templated opening ('Moving a pet from the UK to Russia...') rewritten to lead with the specific fact instead. Routes 6,421 -> 6,446, pages 7,227 -> 7,252. Chunk 81 (Template A, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_79: Chunk 78 DONE (Template C, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block) + content plan Day 31 (South Korea country pillar, delivered as a country-guide upgrade rather than a new blog file; see below). Chunk 78 closed a reciprocal-gap cluster among five Tier B countries not yet paired both ways with the US, Australia and UAE: Uzbekistan, Brunei, Laos, Seychelles and Trinidad and Tobago. US x Uzbekistan (2 routes) + Uzbekistan-to-UK (1, completing the reciprocal pair after chunk 77 built UK-to-Uzbekistan) + UAE x {Brunei, Laos, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago, Uzbekistan} both directions (10) + US x {Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago} (2, completing pairs after Seychelles/Trinidad-to-US already existed) + Australia x {Brunei, Laos, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago, Uzbekistan} both directions (10) = 25 routes. CDC risk-list check (US import side): confirmed Seychelles and Trinidad and Tobago are NOT on CDC's 15 April 2026 high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (standard pathway, no titre test), while Uzbekistan IS on it (full high-risk pathway: CDC Dog Import Form, serology test, CDC-registered facility airport). Routes 6,371 -> 6,396, pages 7,177 -> 7,202. Chunk 79 (Template D, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
